# UC-22.11.65 · Splunk `_audit` Tamper Indicators — Saved Search Deletes and Role Changes

## Description

Alerts on privileged actions that could weaken log integrity—such as deleting saved searches that power PCI dashboards or shrinking roles—supporting protection of audit trails from alteration.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS, PCI-DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes _audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Restrict `_audit` visibility to security admin roles. (2) Forward `_audit` to immutable SIEM archive if required. (3) Map `user` to named individuals only. (4) Incident response playbook for suspected tampering. Indexes required: `index=_audit`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=_audit` `action IN ("delete","edit_user","edit_roles")` — `user`, `object`, `operation`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Restrict `_audit` visibility to security admin roles. (2) Forward `_audit` to immutable SIEM archive if required. (3) Map `user` to named individuals only. (4) Incident response playbook for suspected tampering.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_audit (action="delete" OR operation="delete") earliest=-24h
| where match(object,"(?i)savedsearch|alert|correlation|pci|cde)")
| stats earliest(_time) as first, values(user) as actors by object
| sort first
```

Understanding this SPL

**Splunk `_audit` Tamper Indicators — Saved Search Deletes and Role Changes (PCI DSS Req 10.5.1, 10.2.1.2)** — Alerts on privileged actions that could weaken log integrity—such as deleting saved searches that power PCI dashboards or shrinking roles—supporting protection of audit trails from alteration.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=_audit` `action IN ("delete","edit_user","edit_roles")` — `user`, `object`, `operation`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _audit.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(object,"(?i)savedsearch|alert|correlation|pci|cde)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by object** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (object, actors), timeline (first), single value (daily delete count).

## SPL

```spl
index=_audit (action="delete" OR operation="delete") earliest=-24h
| where match(object,"(?i)savedsearch|alert|correlation|pci|cde)")
| stats earliest(_time) as first, values(user) as actors by object
| sort first
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (object, actors), timeline (first), single value (daily delete count).
